🚀 Beginner EDA to Clean CV + GPU CatBoost
Cabin structure, CryoSleep, spending behavior, group-aware validation, GPU-ready CatBoost, OOF blending, and a fold-averaged submission for predicting Transported.

StratifiedGroupKFold

7-9. Validation and Interpretation
Group-aware CV, ablation, threshold tuning, and feature importance.
Spaceship Titanic is a binary classification problem. For each passenger, the model predicts whether Transported is True or False. The public score is accuracy.

### 4.1 Rule-Based Imputation Audit

Some missing values can be filled with structural rules before the general model pipeline runs. These rules are intentionally conservative: they use passenger metadata and observed feature relationships, not the target label.

    subtitle='Correlation is only a first screen; nonlinear models can still use threshold-like patterns such as zero spend.',
Correlation captures simple linear association. Tree models can also use nonlinear patterns such as zero spend versus any spend.

Validation works best when train and test come from similar distributions. We cannot see the test labels, but we can compare feature distributions. Large train/test differences are a warning that local validation may not match the Kaggle score perfectly.

Group-aware validation keeps passengers from the same `PassengerId` group inside the same fold. `StratifiedGroupKFold` preserves class balance while reducing group leakage. | Soft Voting Ensemble | Averaged scikit-learn probabilities. | | CatBoost | Categorical interactions and raw surname, GPU-ready on Kaggle P100. | | LightGBM + fold-safe target encoding | Compact categorical encoding without full-train target leakage. | | OOF blend | Transparent probability-weight search using out-of-fold predictions. |
CV note: count features use target-free train+test covariates such as surname and cabin counts. The score is therefore closer to a leaderboard-style transductive estimate than a fully strict future-data estimate.
